rag_rules: Repair AGREE diagnostics by ensuring visible identifiers are used and scope rules are followed for component type vs. implementation annexes.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model or its dependencies.
generic_rules: Validate that all AGREE expressions use consistent types and refer to declared variables or helpers.
When fixing AGREE errors, prefer removing invalid clauses over adding new architecture elements.
Ensure AGREE contracts only reference features and ports visible in their current scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be placed in the correct annex scope: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
AGREE scalar types are int, real, and bool; avoid using AADL property names directly as variables.
anti_patterns: Avoid generating AGREE expressions that reference unresolved identifiers or types not declared in the model.
Do not attempt to fix diagnostics by introducing new package or data type references not present in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about empty packages or parsing errors
